title: Run differential and round-trip tests
description: Compile a program corpus at different optimization levels (and parse/print round-trip) and report any behavioral divergence.
body: Use when validating a compiler change for correctness. Actions: run a corpus of programs through the compiler at multiple optimization levels, compare observable output (and parse/print round-trip idempotence), and report any program whose behavior diverges between levels with the minimal reproducing input. Do not declare a pass correct from a single hand-picked example. This skill exposes capability only and must not run untrusted corpus programs without runtime sandboxing/permission. Validation signals include identical observable results across opt levels on the whole corpus. Risk boundary: any divergence between optimized and unoptimized output is a likely miscompilation; flag it as blocking rather than dismissing it as a test-corpus issue.
